Reentrancy in DeFiOperator.batchRebalance(address[],uint256[],uint256[]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#76-88):
	External calls:
	- require(bool,string)(tokenA.transferFrom(users[i],address(this),amountsA[i]),batch step1 failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#79)
	- require(bool,string)(tokenA.approve(address(router),amountsA[i]),batch step2 failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#80)
	- amounts = router.swapExactTokensForTokens(amountsA[i],minOuts[i],path,users[i],block.timestamp + 60) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#84)
	- tokenB.transfer(users[i],amounts[amounts.length - 1]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#85)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- RebalanceCompleted(users[i],amounts[amounts.length - 1]) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#86)

Reentrancy in DeFiOperator.executeMultiStepSwap(uint256,uint256,uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#46-66):
	External calls:
	- require(bool,string)(tokenA.transferFrom(msg.sender,address(this),amountIn),step1: transferFrom failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#48)
	- require(bool,string)(tokenA.approve(address(router),amountIn),step2: approve failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#49)
	- amountsAB = router.swapExactTokensForTokens(amountIn,minOutA,pathAB,address(this),block.timestamp + 60) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#53)
	- require(bool,string)(tokenB.approve(address(router),amountB),step4: approve B failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#57)
	- amountsBC = router.swapExactTokensForTokens(amountB,minOutB,pathBC,address(this),block.timestamp + 60) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#61)
	- tokenC.transfer(msg.sender,amountC) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#63)
	Event emitted after the call(s):
	- SwapExecuted(msg.sender,amountIn,amountC) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#64)

DeFiOperator.executeMultiStepSwap(uint256,uint256,uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#46-66) uses timestamp for comparisons
	Dangerous comparisons:
	- require(bool,string)(amountsAB.length > 0,step3: swap failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#54)
	- require(bool,string)(amountB >= minOutA,step3: slippage) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#56)
	- require(bool,string)(tokenB.approve(address(router),amountB),step4: approve B failed) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#57)

DeFiOperator.approveAndSwap(address,uint256,address,address[],uint256) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#68-74) ignores return value by IERC20(tokenIn).approve(customRouter,amount) (../../../../../tmp/sentinel_static_lfi0ue6b.sol#71)
Contracts have owners with privileged rights to perform admin tasks and need to be trusted to not perform malicious updates or drain funds.
Define and use `constant` variables instead of using literals. If the same constant literal value is used multiple times, create a constant state variable and reference it throughout the contract.

Solc compiler version 0.8.20 switches the default target EVM version to Shanghai, which means that the generated bytecode will include PUSH0 opcodes. Be sure to select the appropriate EVM version in case you intend to deploy on a chain other than mainnet like L2 chains that may not support PUSH0, otherwise deployment of your contracts will fail.

Avoid `require` / `revert` statements in a loop because a single bad item can cause the whole transaction to fail. It's better to forgive on fail and return failed elements post processing of the loop
Check for `address(0)` when assigning values to address state variables.
State variables that are only changed in the constructor should be declared immutable to save gas. Add the `immutable` attribute to state variables that are only changed in the constructor
Function returns a value but it is ignored. Consider checking the return value.
ERC20 functions may not behave as expected. For example: return values are not always meaningful. It is recommended to use OpenZeppelin's SafeERC20 library.
Consider using a specific version of Solidity in your contracts instead of a wide version. For example, instead of `pragma solidity ^0.8.0;`, use `pragma solidity 0.8.0;`
